name: '93 Classic T. Rex
description: This bright red and striped variant of the Tyrannosaurus rex has brought children lots of fun (and fear) over the years!

name: Acrocanthops
description: If this hybrid were a dog it would be bull terrier. Its lean and muscular body tapers down towards its hips, giving it maximum power when lunging forward.

name: Acrocanthosaurus
description: Acrocanthosaurus was the largest carnivore in North America during the Early Cretaceous period. This theropod evolved with massive holes - called fenestra - on the sides of its skull, easing the weight of its 51 inch long head.

name: Aenocyonyx
description: Like modern hyenas, Aenocyonyx packs are matriarchal: females lead hunts, and get prime cuts of the kill!

name: Aerospinosaurus
description: Aerospinosaurus looks slow, but the lighter bones inherited from its flying ancestor allow it to move rapidly while chasing prey. The mutualism exhibited by the Ile Saint-Hubert Spinosaurus is similarly present with the Aerospinosaurus, often finding itself hunting in pair with local pterosaurs!

name: Aerotitan
description: The giant flyer Aerotitan is one of the first azhdarchids discovered in South America, offering rare fossil evidence for the global distribution of these powerful pterosaurs.

name: Ailurarctos
description: Ailurarctos is an extinct genus of Panda! This creature lived in China in the Late Miocene, around 8 million years ago.

name: Alacranix
description: Like modern scorpions, the hyaline layer of the alacranix's exoskeleton reacts to UV light, causing this giant arachnid to glow in the dark!

name: Alankydactylus
description: Although small in size and cute as can be, Alankydactylus can defend themselves effectively! They don't hesitate to swoop down on their aggressors, tearing at their faces with their sharp claws and beaks. Beware!

name: Alankyloceratops
description: Alankyloceratops has 17 unique muscles in its neck and wings. As these muscles develop, the superhybrid is better able to support its large head in flight. This fact helps specialists who study the creature: they use the height of the head in flight to determine the age of individuals!

name: Alankylosaurus
description: Fusion of pterosaur and ankylosaurid DNA has given this flying hybrid uniquely strong defenses.

name: Alanqa
description: The lance-like bill of the Alanqa has been compared to the bill of the Great Blue Heron and other large, fish-eating birds. The Alanqa is particulary adept at using this bill to strike quickly at its targets and escape before retaliation.

name: Albertocevia
description: The mottled stripes of the cold-blooded Albertocevia are a good indicator of its mood. Never approach when these stripes are at their brightest blue. This is when the superhybrid will be most aggressive!

name: Albertosaurus
description: Though this tyrannosaurid is a top predator in its own ecosystems, the Albertosaurus is much smaller than its relative the Tyrannosaurus rex. Paleontologists believe this may have led the Albertosaurus to work together in packs like wolves. More observation is required.

name: Albertospinos
description: With its powerful build and aquatic hunting adaptations, Albertospinos is an ideal river predator. This superhybrid's stripe patterns provide camouflage for rushing water, and groups of Albertospinos have even been observed battling for aquatic territories like modern crocodiles! Watch out for the 'river dragon' Albertospinos!

name: Allodrigues
description: Like the Alloraptor, the Allodrigues hunts alone, stalking prey it can easily transport back to its flock. Though small, Allodrigues are fiercely territorial, competing with other flocks for the best hunting grounds!

name: Alloraptor
description: A combination of problem-solving and stealth hunting skills makes this hybrid an unforgiving predator. The Alloraptor prefers to hunt alone, and normally targets smaller prey that can easily be transported back to its pack.

name: Allosaurus
description: The Allosaurus is a highly active hunter, using its jaw like a hatchet to slash at prey.

name: Allosaurus Gen2
description: This mature Allosaurus has developed great skill as an ambush hunter. This may be why it is more difficult to find in the wild than the common Allosaurus.

name: Allosinosaurus
description: Allosinosaurus is highly territorial. It can often be observed fighting for alpha position in herds, similar to a modern water buffalo. Geneticists believe the carnivore DNA spliced into this hybrid's genome amplified the Sinoceratops' already territorial tendencies.

name: Amargasaurus
description: At 30 feet, the Amargasaurus is smaller than most sauropods, but its sturdy legs allow it to run faster than many of its relatives. Its speed also allows the Amargasaurus to escape stalking predators like the T. rex.

name: Amargocephalus
description: Amargocephalus boasts both the bone scutes of the Euoplocephalus and the dorsal spikes of the Amargasaurus. The skeletal density of this hybrid makes it one of the heaviest dinosaurs relative to its size.

name: Ampelorex
description: This predatory sauropod uses its physical similarity to other long-necked herbivores as a hunting advantage. It has been observed blending into herds of apatosaurs and ampelosaurs in order to pick off weak herd members from within.

name: Ampelosaurus
description: Ampelosaurus, a Late Cretaceous titanosaur, was one of the few sauropods with bony armor, called osteoderms, providing extra protection. It relied on its massive size and armor for defense rather than speed.

name: Amphicyon
description: Amphicyon is renowned for being one of the most adaptable mammals to exist. For 15 million years of the Miocene, these four legged carnivores thrived across Europe, Asia, North America, and Africa.

name: Andrewsarchus
description: The only known specimen from Andrewsarchus is a skull that was discovered in Mongolia in 1923. InGen found a way to clone the creature, and the results revealed its tough and fatty skin, which supports the popular theory that Andrewsarchus is a relative of the hippopotamus.

name: Andrewtherium
description: Many believe Andrewtherium's hybrid parent Andrewsarchus is a genetic relative of the modern day hippopotamus. The unique behaviour of the Andrewtherium may support this theory. When wading or crossing waters, this hybrid naturally uses its short trunk like a snorkel, keeping it above water even when the rest of its head is submerged!

name: Andrewtodon
description: Andrewtodon's skin is 4 inches thick and patched with metal-like scales. Engineers had to redesign drone dart sensors because the originals were not strong enough to scan through the creature's tough exterior.

name: Andrewtops
description: Andrewtops has a unique skill among the creatures of Jurassic World. Like the modern Argentinian Tegu, this hybrid can raise its own body temperature, becoming a temporarily warm-blooded reptile! Specialists believe this capability may come from its mammalian ancestry.

name: Angel
description: Although she's from the same family, Angel is more fiery than her brother Rebel. Be careful not to provoke her, as she won't hesitate to trample your ego... Literally as well as figuratively!

name: Animantarx
description: Animantarx means 'living citadel!' This nodosaurid is so named for the unique shape of its armoured scutes, projecting from the creature's back like a medieval keep!

name: Ankylocodon
description: The Ankylocodon uses the club tail it inherited from the Ankylosaurus much like a beaver would by slapping the ground and water during both communication and combat.

name: Ankylodactylus
description: The flying forager Ankylodactylus can be found roosting in the high branches of trees or hunting for fruit and insects on the forest floor. The pigments of its armor change from orange to deep red depending on its diet.

name: Ankylodicurus
description: Ankylodicurus is an opportunistic hybrid that can survive in both desert and wetland habitats. It rarely eats, but when it does it gorges on plant-based substances.

name: Ankylomoloch
description: The cranial dome of the Ankylomoloch is believed to be the strongest of any Jurassic World creature. This is because this dome must hold up not only against headbutts, but the powerful club of other Anklylosaurs!

name: Ankylos Lux
description: Glowing spines decorate the cranium, spine and tail of the armored Ankylos lux. At night, congregations of these creatures have been observed using their bioluminescent tails to draw complex patterns in the air. More study is required to understand what these displays could signal.

name: Ankylosaurus
description: A full-grown Ankylosaurus requires over 130 lbs of ferns a day. That's as much forage as an African elephant.

name: Ankylosaurus Gen 2
description: The unique color of this Ankylosaurus doesn't mean it's any less fortified. Even the eyelids of the Ankylosaurus are armored!

name: Ankyntrosaurus
description: This heavily armored horned lizard is a gentle giant. Along with its defensive spikes, it inherited herd behavior from the Kentrosaurus. It grazes in groups larger than any other Ankylosaur.

name: Antarctopelta
description: This Cretaceous creature is named after its home in Antarctica. Like Nodosaurus, Antarctopelta is one of the few members of the Ankylosaurid family to have no tail club.

name: Antarctovenator
description: This superhybrid is known for relentlessly destroying wetland ecosystems. We aren’t able to contain it within Sanctuaries, and so alternative groups are taking unauthorized actions to prevent irreversible damage to the environment. Should a life form be subject to extinction for its destructive habits? Time will tell.

name: Anurognathus
description: The tiny Anurognathus is a very unique pterosaur. With a rounded skull, giant eyes, and bat-like wings, the Anurognathus is ideally adapted for the night-time aerial acrobatics that come with insect hunting. Even more impressive, the furry filaments on the wings of these tiny creatures help their wingbeats stay silent when approaching prey, an advantage shared by many modern day owls!

name: Apatoceratops
description: This armored sauropod combines nodosaur, sauropod and ceratopsian genetics. Weak only on their underbelly, they have been observed bedding down when threatened, exposing only their armor to predators!

name: Apatosaurus
description: The long tail of Apatosaurus isn't just for balance. This herbivore can whip its tail fast enough to break the sound barrier, or give an attacking carnivore a stern rebuke.

name: Aquicomilus
description: With keen eyesight and an eerie, almost bird-like screech, Aquicomilus is a ghostly terrestrial predator of open plains and dense forests alike!

name: Aquignathus
description: The Aquignathus is a rapid, highly intelligent flock creature that can swim in shallow water and move fast on land, using highly intelligent pack hunter tactics to hunt down prey in every environment they can find themeslves in, thanks to the introduction of Ardontognathus DNA to the Aquiraptor.

name: Aquilamimus
description: The call of an Aquilamimus sounds like the modern day Golden Eagle, but is significantly louder. By flexing its vocal cords it can let out a cry up of to 100 decibels. That’s louder than a motorcycle engine!

name: Aquilops
description: This early cretaceous creature is known for its social behavior and crested skull. A herbivorous species often preyed upon by larger carnivores, it cries out for other flock members when in danger, showing resilience when alongside its flockmates.

name: Aquilosaurus
description: With powerful legs and an incredibly lightweight frame, Aquilosaurus is known for its ability to leap incredible distances. Its keen eyesight, inherited from its Haast eagle lineage, allows it to track even the slightest movements.

name: Aquilotae
description: This fusion of Aquilosaurus and Panthera Blytheae is perfectly adapted to hunt its prey on both the open plain and in cold, mountain environments!

name: Aquiraptor
description: The introduction of Graciliraptor DNA to the Aquisaurus highly developed their pack instincts, allowing them to perform maneuvers more complex than basic flock behavior, including tail feather communication!

name: Aquisaurus
description: Aquisaurus diversifies its diet by using its horns to burrow for roots and tubers in addition to eating grass. These sharp instruments helps to protect against frontal attacks while keeping a lithe enough body to avoid predators' attacks and flee if need be.

name: Arambourgiania
description: The Arambourgiania has an estimated wingspan of 10 meters (30 feet) or more! Like other azhdarcid pterosaurs, this giant flyer is skilled at both hunting from the air and foraging on the ground.

name: Archaeopteryx
description: Once thought to be the earliest bird that ever lived, Archaeopteryx's name means "ancient wing." Though tiny, this bird plays a giant role in the history of science, helping to prove the theory of evolution only two years after Darwin's On the Origin of Species was first published!

name: Archaeotherium
description: Archaeotherium was a ravenous omnivore. It had wide cheekbones, teeth unlike any modern creature, and was roughly the same size as a modern bull. It's no surprise Archaeotherium translates to “ancient beast”!

name: Arctalces
description: The antlers of the Arctalces grow slowly, never reaching the impressive size of those of its hybrid parent Cervalces. Specialists believe this is because, unlike a herbivore, the Arctalces does not need these antlers for jousting or defense. It prefers to use its claws and sharp teeth in such moments!

name: Arctocanis
description: Although it primarily hunts alone, Arctocanis is known to gather in small packs to take down larger, more dangerous prey, using cooperative tactics reminiscent of its wolf lineage.

name: Arctodus
description: The Arctodus, or short-faced bear, may be the largest mammalian carnivore to ever live on land in North America! This Pleistocene predatory scavenger could grow up to 12 feet tall! How do scientists know this? They've found Arctodus claw marks 15 feet up a cave wall in Missouri!

name: Arctops
description: Arctops is a vicious hunter who thrived in South Africa during the Late Permian period. This carnivore’s name means ‘Bear Face’ because the shape of its skull resembles that of a modern day brown bear.

name: Arctovasilas
description: The solitary 'bear king' Arctovasilas is a viciously territorial predator. After hunting, Arctovasilas caches its kill in an icy cavern den, patroling the perimeter constantly. No specialist has yet set foot in one of these caverns to see what remains they might contain...

name: Ardentismaxima
description: Ardentismaxima's relative Brachiosaurus is well known for its booming calls. The majestic Ardentismaxima can project its voice even further, likely aided by the reverberation of its frill.

name: Ardontognathus
description: Specialists have described the Ardontognathus as an 'everything eater.' Comfortable on the ground, in trees, or sliding through shallow water, the Ardontognathus has already become an invasive species in several habitats.

name: Ardontosaurus
description: Some dinosaur trainers compare the Ardontosaurus to a gigantic marine iguana. This sauropod hybrid's long neck and narrow skull is expertly adapted to foraging on marsh grass and underwater weeds. The creature's sail also makes it an excellent sun-bather, allowing it to stay warmer while feeding longer than other creatures its size!

name: Argentavis
description: This soaring Cenozoic creature is among the largest birds to ever fly! With a wingspan of 6 meters or more, it covers hundreds of kilometers per day in search of carrion. Specialists have even observed Argentavis using its aerial advantage to chase powerful predators like Smilodon from their kills!

name: Argenteryx
description: What this tiny avian hybrid lacks in size it makes up for in craftiness! Specialists have observed this flyer using pebbles to break the shells of nutritious eggs and large insects, just like the modern Egyptian vulture! Perhaps Argenteryx inherited the trait from its Argentavis ancestor...

name: Argentinosaurus
description: This relative of the Apatosaur specializes in picking leaves from forest canopies. It also uses its impressive size for stomping attacks against predators.

name: Armbruster's Wolf
description: This ancient canine was an apex predator of the Pleistocene epoch. Specialists familiar with the creature have taken to shortening its 'Armbruster' binomial to 'Aster.' Meaning 'star' in Greek, it's a fitting title for this night sky colored wolf!

name: Atrocimoloch
description: Atrocimoloch means 'cruel demon' or 'cruel god.' The name suits this sinister dromaeosaurid bred from lethal Atrociraptor DNA.

name: Atrocodistis
description: The feet, claws, head, and tail of Atrocodistis can all become weapons, but its most curious feature is its occipital horn. Mature Atrocodistis bond by sharpening these horns against each other, a unique behaviour specialists have only observed in this hybrid.

name: Atrocomaxima
description: Conflicting instincts make the Atrocomaxima a tough one to raise. The instinct to go for the throat and head remains, but its multitude of natural weapons such as horns and claws are not suited for that purpose. Instead, it will rapidly ram its prey to knock it to the ground and then attack the neck like its Constrictoraptor ancestor.

name: Atromolistis
description: Atromolistis is a stalking hunter, using its shadowy blue-black scales to hunt under cover of darkness. This Atrociraptor hybrid will chase down larger prey without hesitation!

name: Australotitan
description: Australotitan is a Titanosaur and the largest known Australian dinosaur. Its thighbone alone measures 7 feet!

name: Bajadasaurus
description: The long neck vertebrae on this small sauropod are curved forward. This distinct curve sets the Bajadasaurus apart from its close relative, the Amargasaurus.

name: Bajatonodon
description: This superhybrid is known to pick up foliage in its long neck vertebrae by brushing against trees. Like the creatures it is fused from, the Bajatonodon is social, and will allow a variety of sauropoda and hadrosauridae creatures to eat from the foliage caught in its neck vertebrae.

name: Baryonyx
description: The thumb claws of Baryonyx are over a foot long, giving this crocodile-faced carnivore a deadly grip.

name: Baryonyx Gen 2
description: Both GENs of the Baryonyx exhibit behavior very similar to modern crocodiles. These dinosaurs clap their jaws and splash in the water to communicate.

name: Baryosaurus
description: The Baryosaurus was engineered to try and fix the vision issues experienced by the Baryothus. It succeeded, in a way. Instead of sharpening its vision, the Baryosaurus sharpened every other one of its senses, allowing it full 360 degree "vision" of its environment. Mixed with the predatory instincts of the Sarcosaurus, it is practically impossible to surprise or ambush this dinosaur.

name: Baryothus
description: One of the most unusual traits of Baryothus is inherited from Leucistic Baryonyx. A specialized visual filtering system gives Baryothus excellent low-light vision, perfectly adapted to murky rivers and floodplains, making it a master ambusher in these terrains.

name: Baryotor
description: The Baryotor's nasal horn, while not useful for attacking, is a natural visual aid to the otherwise vision-impaired creature, almost like an aiming tool. Its hind legs are also more enduring as it can reposition itself and pounce onto prey immediately. A Baryotor entrenched in its natural habitat is all but impossible to remove.

name: Becklejara
description: Improving on the ambush tactics of its hybrid ancestor Becklespinax, the Becklejara is known to 'play possum'-- feigning weakness in order to lure prey close before attacking!

name: Becklerizaurus
description: Becklerizaurus' enlarged forelimbs allow it to deliver raking swipes that destabilize prey before closing in with a crushing bite. Yet when not hunting, Becklerizaurus can often be found upright, using its strong claws to strip bark or rake soil for roots.

name: Becklolyth
description: Becklolyth’s feathers aren’t just for show—they are a direct reflection of its social rank and development! Hatchlings and juveniles start with dull, sparse feathers, but as they mature, their plumage darkens and develops distinct coloration based on their standing within the group.

name: Becklophosaurus
description: Becklophosaurus has been observed stealing food from other predators using clever distractions. Some individuals have even been seen mimicking the calls of smaller dinosaurs, luring them into traps before striking.

name: Beelzebufo
description: Commonly called the 'devil frog,' Beelzebufo lived in Madagascar in the Cretaceous period. With a large and strong jaw, Beelzebufo may have even preyed upon infant dinosaurs!

name: Beta
description: A 4-foot baby raptor, but growing fast, Beta is an exact clone of her mother, Blue!

name: Big Eatie
description: Mother to a young tyrannosaurus, Big Eatie is ready to crush anything that may threaten her offspring. Her jaws are able to rend through any other dinosaur's defenses.

name: Blonde
description: Blonde is a Becklespinax, a stealthy predator species which uses its agility to ambush small dinosaurs and mammals, striking with sudden bursts of speed.

name: Blue
description: On Jurassic World, Blue was the beta of Owen Grady's raptor pack. Unlike other Jurassic World dinosaurs, Blue makes conscious decisions about her allegiances and the company she keeps.

name: Borealopelta
description: Borealopelta means 'Northern Shield.' Discovered in Alberta, Canada, this ankylosaur is remarkable for the large bony spikes that protrude from its shoulders.

name: Brachiosaurus
description: The deep, resonating calls of this social and curious herbivore can carry for miles.

name: Brontolasmus
description: This hybrid inherited its size and appetite from its mighty relatives, the Brontotherium and the Elasmotherium. Subsisting on a diet of inland grass, one Brontolasmus can clear up to 5 acres in a single day.

name: Brontotherium
description: Brontotherium resembles a giant rhinoceros with two horns instead of one. This gigantic armored herbivore stands 8 feet (2.5 meters) tall at the shoulders, almost the size of an African forest elephant!

name: Brunette
description: The beastly Brunette is a female Becklespinax famous for her destructive actions in Jurassic World: Chaos Theory!

name: Bumpy
description: Born with one large horn, Dr. Wu considered Bumpy a genetic imperfection. It took the Camp Cretaceous campers to show how special Bumpy is, the first 'perfectly flawed' creature to come out of Jurassic World!

name: Carbonemys
description: Like most members of the Podocnemididae family, Carbonemys has a shell streamlined for swimming. The Carbonemys' shell can grow to nearly 2 meters in length, making it one of the largest turtles in the world!

name: Carbotoceratops
description: The fusion of the Carbonemys and the Nasutoceratops created what geneticists coined “The Most Unhuggable Turtle”. This hybrid uses her shell spikes defensively, and keeps them sharp by swimming between boulders.

name: Carcharodontosaurus
description: The serrated, recurved teeth of Carcharodontosaurus earned it the name 'shark-toothed lizard.' Roughly 40 feet in length, this giant theropod stalked North Africa from 99 to 94 million years ago.

name: Carnotarkus
description: Though an herbivore, the carnivore DNA of this superhybrid makes it highly territorial.

name: Carnotaurus
description: The Carnotaurus always wolfs down its food. This theropod knows that its rival Tyrannosaurus is an opportunistic hunter and could show up to take over the carcass.

name: Centroceratops
description: The bright spots on the Centroceratops serve as a warning to predators. While usually found on venomous creatures, this is yet another natural tool in this ceratopsid to dissuade predators. Small horns surround its crest to further dissuade predators from even trying to chase after it.

name: Centrolophus Lux
description: The investigation into the Parasaurolophus Lux's natural bioluminescence ended with more questions than answers. it also yielded the Centrolophus Lux, who shows bioluminescent stripes and a spot at the top of its crest. These can end up so distracting to potential predators that they stare long enough for the Centrolophus Lux to charge them in return.

name: Centrosaurus
description: A frilled, horned herbivore that is smaller in stature than other ceratopsids. Its horn can curve inwards or outwards, but whichever way it points, whatever it rams will undoubtedly be shook up.

name: Ceramagnus
description: Ceramagnus is the largest Ceratopsian creature to appear on the Map. It is speculated to weigh about 15,000 lbs, and its largest horn is roughly 16 feet long.

name: Ceranosaurus
description: Like modern day elephants, Ceranosaurus can signal between groups with infrasound and seismic vibration, detecting the latter both through their sensitive foot pads and the unique shape of their cranial crest!

name: Cerastegotops
description: This crested fusion of Stegosaur and Ceratosaur bloodlines is remarkable for its mottled burgundy color.

name: Ceratosaurus
description: This powerful theropod can be distinguished by the bladed horn on its snout. Slightly smaller than the T. rex, Ceratosaurus is an aggressive predator, using its strength and resilience to attack prey head on!

name: Ceratosaurus Gen 2
description: The cranial and eyebrow crests of the Ceratosaurus GEN 2 are more pronounced than those of the Ceratosaurus. These crests serve as eye protection from sun, allowing Ceratosaurus GEN 2 better vision than many other dinosaurs!

name: Cervalces
description: The megafaunal cervid Cervalces is an ancestor of many modern deer. The reasons of this majestic herbivore's extinction still remain unknown. It might have been over-hunted by ancient humans, or pushed out of its ecological niche by other, more modern herbivores! Further observation is required.

name: Charlie
description: Charlie is the youngest of Owen's raptor pack. Her green color pattern comes from the Iguana DNA infused within her genome.

name: Clever Girl
description: An ambush hunter, this Velociraptor has been known to work with pack-mates posing as bait to take out its (sometimes human) prey!

name: Coelhaast
description: Unlike its hybrid parent Coelurosauravus, the Coelhaast can not only glide on its wings, but also flap them to achieve true flight! It inherited this trait from the powerful Haast Eagle!

name: Coelurosauravus
description: This prehistoric flying lizard is actually more of a glider! Thin bones growing from the side of Coelurosauravus' body support wing-like membranes which allow this tiny creature to ride the air when leaping between trees!

name: Compsocaulus
description: Some specialists call this flocking hybrid 'the arrowhead,' for obvious reasons. Make sure to keep the Compsocaulus in your 'quiver,' as this tiny cunning creature is especially useful against other flocks!

name: Compsognathus
description: Although most of Peter Ludlow's expedition on Isla Sorna fell victim to the island's Raptors, some missing team members are also suspected to have been hunted by roving flocks of these "Compies," as they are often called.

name: Compsognathus Gen 2
description: Compsognathus is a crafty carnivore that loves to work together with other members of its flock. Although they prefer smaller prey, when trained correctly this team of tiny terrors can take down creatures much larger in size!

name: Compsoraptor
description: Compsoraptor have been observed producing a range of communicative chirps and growls that assist them in highly coordinated hunts. Some specialists believe each flock may even be capable of producing its own unique communicative system!

name: Compsovenator
description: The Compsovenator species heavily depends on coordinated attacks for its survival. Although individually they may seem frail, when acting collectively, they exhibit a remarkable strength that enables them to overwhelm dinosaurs much larger than themselves!

name: Concakuisaurus
description: Though the neck and chest of Concakuisaurus are quite strong, its jaws are relatively weak. This predator feeds by tearing off large chunks of meat and swallowing it whole.

name: Concatoloch
description: This predatory hadrosaurid hybrid uses the auditory signalling of its crest to deceive other hadrosaur species, drawing them into its hunting range!

name: Concatosaurus
description: Concatosaurus scale-pattern is specifically designed for arid environments, where it camouflages and offsets heat. In this environment, Concatosaurus uses its keen sense of smell to hunt, and may also communicate with other members of the species through pheromones.

name: Concavenator
description: Like a cat, Concavenator has bristles on its arms that allow it to better detect and manipulate prey between its claws.

name: Constrictoraptor
description: Although it does not have the squeezing abilities of its hybrid ancestor Dilophoboa, Constrictoraptor does seem to share its instincts. This powerful predator preferentially attacks the neck and throat of its prey, looking to cut off blood to the brain.

name: Crichtomoloch
description: The facial horns of the Crichtomoloch grow to different dimensions depending on its seniority in herds of other ankylosaurids!

name: Crichtonsaurus
description: This ankylosaur is named for the writer who started it all, Michael Crichton, the author of Jurassic Park!

name: Cryolophosaurus
description: The small cranial crest of Cryolophosaurus was likely used for intra-species identification. Among modern scientists, it earns this theropod the nickname 'Elvisaurus' for its resemblance to the pompadour of Elvis Presley!

name: Dakotacurus
description: Like a modern leopard, this burly sabre-tooth relies on the crushing power of its jaws to take down its prey!

name: Dakotanops
description: Look closely at Dakotanops’ feathers. They are thick and tightly linked together, creating an ultra-light weight protective covering. This hybrid molts once a year to replace feathers damaged by foes.

name: Dakotaraptor
description: Dakotaraptor was 18 feet in length, placing it second to Utahraptor on this list of largest known Dromaeosaurs. With attachment points for pennaceous feathers on its forearm bones, Dakotaraptor is also one of few dinosaurus confirmed to have feathers.

name: Darwezopteryx
description: The powerful teeth of this pterosaur hybrid make it excellent at catching fish. They also offer a strong defense against attackers!

name: Darwinopterus
description: Darwinopterus means "Darwin's Wing". This creature is believed to have evolved from 2 separate suborders of pterosaurs, which supports Charles Darwin's evolutionary theory.

name: Deinocheirus
description: Deinocheirus is an omnivore, using its massive claws to dig and fish.

name: Deinomimus
description: Deinomimus uses its unique colors and large facial frill as both warning coloration and group-signalling! Deinomimus who are more bonded to each other will become more similar in hue!

name: Deinonychus
description: Paleontologists began to consider the possiblity that dinosaurus may have been warm-blooded animals while studying Deinonychus. This study lead to the 'Dinosaur renaissance', a scientific revolution that contradicts the idea that all dinosaurs were reptilian.

name: Deinosuchus
description: Nearly twice the size of modern crocodiles, Deinosuchus is an ambush hunter. It waits motionless in swamps before exploding out of the water with jaws capable of snapping even large dinosaurs in half.

name: Deinotherium
description: This gentle giant is one of the largest mammals to ever walk the earth. Standing 4-5 meters tall, the Deinotherium has a shorter, stockier trunk than modern elephants. Its inverted tusks may seem strange, but this orientation helps the Deinotherium strip bark and break branches from trees during feeding!

name: Deinotops
description: Combining the immense bite force of Deinosuchus with the powerful legs of Arctops, Deinotops thrives both in and out of the water. Unlike most crocodilians, it prefers to lie in wait on muddy riverbanks, using its camouflaged, rough-textured hide to blend in with its surroundings.

name: Delta
description: Each raptor of Owen's pack is unique. Delta was created using more bird DNA than her sisters. This makes her a little more of a problem solver.

name: Diabloceratops
description: Named for the dual spikes emerging from the top of its crest, Diabloceratops means 'devil-horned face.' This ceratopsid is closely related to the Nasutoceratops.

name: Dilophoboa
description: Unlike the massive snake it was fused from, this carnivorous hybrid prefers to hunt on land instead of water. The Dilophoboa is a venomous constrictor. It can spit lethal poison at targets up to 50 feet away.

name: Dilophosaurus
description: Dilophosaurus primarily hunts by ambush, trying to catch victims unawares.

name: Dilophosaurus Gen 2
description: Like its relative, this uniquely colored Dilophosaurus prefers to hunt by ambush than outright attack.

name: Diloracheirus
description: While researchers were expecting the Diloracheirus to bear the Dilophosaurus's trademark frill, this superhybrid instead sports a sharp spinal sail. The stripes on the sail suggest it may be able to change color like the modern chameleon, but this is so-far unobserved by scientists.

name: Diloranosaurus
description: The unique coloring of Diloranosaurus's frill and the added dimensions of its sail give this creature an intimidating edge against its enemies.

name: Dilozorion
description: Don't be fooled by its beautiful, vibrant colors, because the Dilozorion is undoubtedly an apex predator! Capable of incredible leaps thanks to its hind legs, it propels itself, claws outstretched, to tear apart its victim in less time than it takes to say it!

name: Dimetrodon
description: Engineered by Biosyn, the Dimetrodon's ear-piercing loud screech and shrieks are almost deafening as it stalks its prey deep in the underground amber mines of Biosyn Valley.

name: Dimetrodon Gen 2
description: This stockier, more common Dimetrodon is seemingly sluggish until it pounces in pursuit of prey.

name: Dimodactylus
description: The large frill and powerful jaws of this hybrid require an incredibly strong back, which also gives it one of the most powerful wingbeats of all pterosaurs!

name: Dimorphodon
description: Dimorphodon are not generally aggressive but are easily spooked. They are chatty pterosaurs, known to gibber and screech.

name: Diorajasaur
description: Diorajasaur means 'Doubled Rajasaurus'. This incredible superhybrid is more powerful than either the predatory Rajasaurus or the herbivorous Tuojiangosaurus.

name: Diplocaulus
description: The odd shape of the Diplocaulus's skull helps it glide through the water.

name: Diplocaulus Gen 2
description: This genetic variation of the Diplocaulus genome is more abundant in the wild than its relative. Maybe the abundance accounts for its more territorial nature.

name: Diplodocus
description: Even though they are among the largest of all dinosaurs, all Diplodocus hatch out of eggs no larger than a grapefruit.

name: Diplotator
description: The Diplotator is difficult to attack from above due to its large spikes. By rooting itself to the ground with strong claws, the Diplotator forces an enemy to get past its spikes in order to strike it.

name: Diplovenator
description: The Diplovenator is a highly aggressive amphibian. This hybrid has been shown to use its strength to steal the burrows of other amphibious creatures.

name: Dire Wolf
description: About 25% larger than a modern grey wolf, the Dire Wolf specialized in large prey, competing with Smilodon for Pleistocene bison, camel, and deer. This prehistoric predator went extinct when their prey did, in the Pleistocene megafaunal extinction roughly 10,000 years ago!

name: Distortus Rex
description: The Distortus Rex is the result of failed experiments by Jurassic Scientists. Little is known about the many edits this six-limbed creature saw-- but one thing is for sure: it is vicious, angry, and dangerous!

name: Dodo
description: Paleontologists working at Jurassic World didn't need fossils to know what the Dodo looked like. This large, flightless bird didn't go extinct until it was exterminated by humans in the 1600s! Keep collecting DNA so we don't let the Dodo down again!

name: Dodocevia
description: Don't let the lack of feathers fool you! This fearsome Dodo-hybrid is no plucked turkey. Its razor beak is even more adept at breaking through tough exteriors than its hybrid parent Inostrancevia's saber teeth.

name: Doedicurus
description: Doedicurus is pronounced Day-dih-core-us and means "pestle tail". Fractures on the fossils of these ancient armadillos prove that they used their tails like pestles to crush each other during duels.

name: Draco Intrepidus
description: Draco Intrepidus means 'fearless dragon.' The name suits this headstrong theropod! Draco Intrepidus uses its teeth for most attacks, but is known to also deliver brutal headbutts when cornered!

name: Draco Lux
description: The solitary Draco lux is believed to only exhibit its full bioluminescent capacities when threatened, drawing opponents towards its most dangerous asset-- its powerful crest!

name: Dracoceratops
description: Some experts compare the Dracoceratops to a bighorn sheep. The comparison suits the hybrid, since the combination of the Triceratops' crest and the Dracorex's reinforced cranium give this creature an incredibly powerful ramming attack.

name: Dracoceratosaurus
description: The padding on the bottom of this biped's feet allows it to move quietly. In fact, Dracoceratosaurus is so stealthy that it is seldom detected by other creatures, and has no known predators.

name: Dracorex
description: The flat head of Dracorex is covered with pointed spikes, which prevents predators from trying to attack it head-on.

name: Dracorex Gen 2
description: Dracorex is a social creature that prefers groups to solitary behavior. It is possible both GENs of this creature co-habitated in their prehistoric environments.

name: Dracovenator
description: Dracovenator is a swift bipedal predator. Large enough to take on powerful opponents and swift enough to evade their attacks, Dracovenator uses its Dodge and Distraction to remain on the attack!

name: Dreadactylus
description: The tiny Dreadactylus may not have the impressive size of its hybrid parent Dreadnoughtus, but they do seem to share some affinities! Flocks of Dreadactylus have been observed congregating in the air around massive Dreadnoughtus like plover birds on crocodiles!

name: Dreadnoughtus
description: The towering Dreadnoughtus measures an incredible 85 feet long and weighs 65 tons. The name Dreadnoughtus means 'fear nothing', because herbivores can be pretty tough too!

name: Dsungaia
description: This mysterious hybrid is incredibly shy and likes to nest in small, secluded groups. Their haunting calls can be heard echoing for miles.

name: Dsungaripterus
description: The curved snout of the Dsungaripterus helps it to collect the shellfish that serve as one of its dietary staples. The strongest of the counter-attack specialized pterosaurs, Dsungaripterus can increase its damage over mutiple attacks.

name: Dsungascorpios
description: Unlike the stingers of most scorpions, the tail of Dsungascorpios bears no venom. Instead, the creature's venom is contained in the yellow barbs on its back and tail, which can paralyze at the slightest touch!

name: Echo
description: In her youth, Echo battled her sister Blue for pack dominance. She lost, and had to get reconstructive surgery on her jaw after the encounter.

name: Edaphocevia
description: Edaphocevia loosely means 'Glacier Base'. The creatures fused to create this hybrid existed around 300 million years ago, during the Permian period. This period pre-dates all known Ice Ages, and so many fossils from it could be buried beneath glaciers.

name: Edaphosaurus
description: Edaphosaurus was one of the first herbivorous tetrapods to exist. The paleontologist who discovered this creature named it "Pavement Lizard", after noticing its unusually dense tooth distribution.

name: Edmontoguanodon
description: This fusion of two separate hadrosaur bloodlines makes for an incredibly social creature. It can be found interacting with many different hadrosaurs, and even other herbivore species! It is believed the hybridization process further catalyzed the instinct for herd behavior already common in the family.

name: Edmontosaurus
description: Edmontosaurus is fast for its size, running at speeds over 25 miles per hour.

name: Einiasuchus
description: The sharp teeth of this hybrid suggest it’s a meat eater. This herbivore actually uses its incisors to chew through tough plant matter, and defend against the occasional predator.

name: Einiosaurus
description: The Einiosaurus is a relative of the Triceratops. With a large defensive skull plate and a uniquely curved horn, this dinosaur is famous for ramming attacks!

name: Elasmotherium
description: This ancient rhinoceros stands up to 6 feet (2 meters) high at the shoulder, taller than any modern relative. It uses its large horn for foraging, defense, and attracting mates.

name: Enteloceros
description: Enteloceros travel in small groups, but also congregate in mega-herds for migration like Caribou. Specialists believe the antlers on the side of Enteloceros' head help to collect the dinstinct musk of their herd-mates, helping all Enteloceros find their way to their companions within large groups!

name: Entelochops
description: Entelochops is fused from creatures that rely heavily on their powerful jaws for survival. Much to the surprise of predators, Entelochops has one of the most effective bites in all of Jurassic World. This omnivore is not afraid to strike down and feed on creatures who attempt to hunt it.

name: Entelodon
description: Better known as Terminator Pig, this ferocious omnivore settled disputes through jaw-wrestling matches.

name: Entelolania
description: Entelolania is the only member of the tortoise family to have fur. This feature allows the cold-blooded hybrid to survive in mild winter environments.

name: Entelomoth
description: This omnivorous hybrid's acute hearing is the result of ear muscles inherited from both members of its fusion. The Entelomoth can rotate its ears 270 degrees and is able to pick up ultrasonic sounds.

name: Eremocanis
description: Little is known about the elusive Eremocanis. These omnivorous wolf, bear and giant ground sloth hybrids live in small packs at the edge of forest and plain landscapes.

name: Eremoceros
description: Eremoceros loosely translates to “Wilderness Giant”. This hybrid is nocturnal, and the moss that grows between its fur disguises its natural scent, making it difficult for predators to track.

name: Eremotherium
description: Eremotherium was 19 feet long and stood 13 feet tall, making it one of the largest sloths to ever exist. Unlike most ancient sloths, Eremotherium fossils have been excavated in both North and South America, giving it the nickname "Panamerican Ground Sloth."

name: Erlidominus
description: The elusive qualities of the Erlikosaurus and the cloaking abilities of the Indominus rex combine in the Erlidominus to create an incredibly stealthy predator.

name: Erlikogamma
description: The coloring on Erlikogamma's back causes other creatures to confuse it for a herbivorous Erlikosaurus Gen 2. Big mistake! This omnivorous hybrid lives in solitude, and will attack creatures that get too close.

name: Erlikosaurus
description: This incredibly rare dinosaur is intelligent and elusive. Its powerful hearing allows it to avoid predators.

name: Erlikosaurus Gen 2
description: When an attempt to reverse engineer a Phorusrhacos, or 'terror bird' from Erlikosaurus DNA failed, this rare Erlikosaurus was produced.

name: Erlikospyx
description: This hybrid has been shown to be quite social, establishing communal nesting grounds and engaging in complex preening behaviors with other therizinosaurs.

name: Erythrosuchus
description: Although diminuitive among the beasts of Jurassic World, Erythrosuchus was the largest predator of early Triassic South Africa!

name: Estemmenosuchus
description: Meet the Estemmenosuchus, a colossus endowed with unique characteristics! With its distinctive horns, it certainly impressed its peers during spectacular displays. Will you dare to confront this giant of the past?

name: Eucladoceros
description: Eucladoceros fossils have been discovered in Europe, the Middle East, and Asia. These fossils reveal the deer’s majestic 8 ft long antlers. The antlers of Eucladoceros are split into great number of tines, which helps distinguish their fossils from those of its relative, Megaloceros.

name: Euoplocephalus
description: Euoplocephalus means 'well-armed head.' The name comes from the thick armor around the face of this ankylosaurid.

name: Fukuimimus
description: Fukuimimus means 'Fukui mimic,' a fitting name for this hadrosaur hybrid in a feathered costume! Mostly grey-blue as hatchlings, the brilliant yellow colorations of the Fukuimimus develop as they mature.

name: Fukuiraptor
description: Discovered in the Fukui prefecture of Japan, Fukuiraptor is a member of the enigmatic clade 'Megaraptora.' These are a group of medium theropods resembling both large carnivores like tyrannosaurids and the smaller, more slender raptors.

name: Fukuisaurus
description: Discovered in the Fukui Prefecture of Japan, the peaceful Fukuisaurus is a relatively small ornithomimid. It measures roughly 4.5 meters in length, and weighs in at 400 kilograms!

name: Fukuitops
description: While remaining a herbivore, Fukuitops has inherited from its Deinosuchus ancestor devious tricks to scare away predators and attack them while they flee. An increase in carrion-eaters in a given area is a good indicator that a Fukuitops is nesting nearby.

name: Gallimimus
description: Gallimimus is among the largest of the ornithomimosaurs, or what experts call the ostrich mimic dinosaurs.

name: Geminideus
description: Geminideus are usually found in pairs, appearing at dusk on the fringes of groups of other sauropods. Remarkably, these mysterious titans split from their partners as night falls, travelling alone until they reunite in locations unknown to specialists.

name: Geminititan
description: This superhybrid is Jurassic World's first omnivorous Sauropod. It can be observed using its long neck to snatch low-flying pterosaurs from the air, just like the yellow-red rat snakes of the Yucatan take down bats for their meals.

name: Geosternbergia
description: The blue crested Geosternbergia is among the largest pterosaurs to ever fly! It has a wingspan of up to 20 feet!

name: Ghost
description: One of four lethal Atrociraptors, Ghost is a pale white carnivore designed to be deadly and trained to attack on command.

name: Giganotosaurus
description: This Cretaceous period theropod is the largest known terrestrial carnivore. The Biosyn Engineered Giganotosaurus measures approximately 43 feet in length and can reach speeds of up to 30 mph!

name: Giganspinoceratops
description: Although the size of Giganspinoceratops dorsal crest may change throughout its life, its real distinguishing marks are its bony spines. Spines near the neck grow as the creature ages, and their size may represent status within the herd.

name: Gigantspinosaurus
description: This giant spined lizard discovered in China is remarkable for the large shoulder spines it used to guard its flanks!

name: Giganyx
description: Though Giganyx's vision is poor, studies suggest it is very sensitive to flashing light, movement and scent! This may be an adaptation for nocturnal hunting.

name: Gigaspikasaur
description: The armor of the Gigaspikasaur is highly unique. While most of its body is no more protected than the average dinosaur, its layered spinal scutes and spikes make it resistant to attacks from above.

name: Giraffatitan
description: For some time this near 40 foot tall Sauropod was considered the largest dinosaur to ever live. The discovery of Titanosaurs such as Elaltitan since then have challenged this claim.

name: Glyptoceras
description: Osteoderms like those of the Glyptodon cover the tail of this hybrid. Glyptoceras uses this armored tail to shake the earth in jousting displays and mark trees at the edge of herd territory!

name: Glyptodon
description: Glyptodon is an ancient relative of the modern armadillo. It first appeared during the Pliocene, and for more than 5 million years, Glylptodon thrived on plants, insects, and even carrion.

name: Glyptosavis
description: Thanks to its dorsal spine inherited from the Concavenator, the Glyptosavis can stabilize its running much more easily than its cousins, allowing it to shake off its pursuers with disconcerting ease!

name: Gorgonops
description: Existing roughly 254 million years ago, Gorgonops is the distant ancestor to many of today’s mammals. Some scientists believe that Gorgonops marked an evolutionary transition from "cold" to "warm" blooded creatures.

name: Gorgosaurus
description: This 30 foot, 5000 pound Tyrannosaurid dominates as a terrestrial predator. Its strong legs make it one of the fastest hunters among its relatives, and its strong bite can take down almost any opponent.

name: Gorgosuchus
description: This powerful hybrid can leap 19 feet in the air, just like a panther! The ability helps when going after prey, or over a wall!

name: Gorgotrebax
description: The temperature drops 10 degrees whenever this blood-chilling Apex makes an appearance on the Map. Gorgotrebax is a dominant and combative carnivore. Other creatures are wise to refrain from entering its territory alone.

name: Graciliraptor
description: Graciliraptor, from the Early Cretaceous, was one of the earliest raptors in Asia. The elongated body of this 'slender thief' suggests it was built more for speed than strength, setting it apart from its sturdier cousins like Velociraptor.

name: Gryganyth
description: The crocodile-headed Gryganyth uses a semi-aquatic hunting style. It uses its powerful neck muscles to clamp on and drag its prey into shallow waters where they're more immobilized!

name: Grylenken
description: DNA from two massive predators was fused in order to create this bird with a full set of teeth. Unlike most birds, Grylenken uses its teeth to slice up food before consumption. Its neck is also designed to withstand swallowing up to 70 lbs whole, and its feeding ritual involves tearing large aquatic prey into chunks before devouring. Grylenken’s taste for aquatic life comes from the crocodilian DNA involved in its fusion.

name: Grypolyth
description: This 'mega-crocodile' is larger and more vicious than any comparable modern day creature. With a stronger tail than the Gryposuchus, the Grypolyth is also a remarkably fast swimmer.

name: Gryposuchus
description: Gryposuchus resembles a giant gharial, a type of crocodile with a long, narrow snout. This snout helps the dinosaur catch fish. More research is needed to find out if the unique adaptation helps the creature in other ways.

name: Haast Eagle
description: The Haast Eagle is the largest eagle ever discovered, likely evolving to such size in order to prey on the 500 lb flightles birds inhabiting its New Zealand home.

name: Haast Eagle Gen 2
description: Both GENs of Haast Eagle have a 9 ft wingspan. That's nearly twice the length of a modern day Bald Eagle.

name: Haast Maximus
description: Specialists have taken to calling this InGen engineered Haast Eagle "pouakai" when they see it soaring through the skies. The name comes from a Maori legend of a monstrous bird that sometimes carried off human prey before disappearing into the clouds!

name: Hadros Lux
description: The cause of ghostly glow on Hadros lux is unknown. Specialists tracked the movements of this massive Hadrosaur, and found that it does not graze while in the presence of others. Perhaps its elusive eating habits hold the secret to its spectacular luminosity.

name: Hatzegopteryx
description: Hatzegopteryx was the apex predator of Hateg Island, a prehistoric landform in the Tethys Sea. With no large theropods on the secluded isle, this pterosaur reigned supreme!

name: Herranotor
description: Hybridization with the Spinotahraptor gives the Herranotor additional food options as the lower profile and flexible claws allows it to feast on fish in addition to pursuing other dinosaur prey. Due to this, Herranotor can be found on both coasts and inland in a complex relationship with other local predators.

name: Herrerasaurus
description: The Herrerasaurus is named after the goatherd who initially discovered its fossils. It has a flexible joint in its lower jaw giving its bite the ability to grasp smaller prey or objects. When faced against a competing predator, it relies on constant harassment to keep them at bay.

name: Homalocephale
description: This quick moving pacycephalosaur is remarkable for the flat shape of its cranium. Don't worry, it's still useful for a headbutt!

name: Hydra Boa
description: This gargantuan boa is named after the many-headed serpent slain by Heracles in Greek myth. The name was chosen by InGen scientists due to a special characteristic of the massive snake's scales. Unlike those of other species, the scales of the Hydra Boa's head and neck grow thicker and brighter each time the snake molts, as if the creature were growing a new head each time!

name: Ichthyovenator
description: Unlike other Spinosaurs, the wave-like neural crest of the Ichthyovenator divides into two separate sails.

name: Iguanodon
description: This bulky herbivore can switch from quadripedal to bipedal motion, making it easier to reach leaves on trees. Its flexible toes also helps it grip plant matter.

name: Imperatosuchus
description: Careful when visiting rocky rivers, the Imperatosuchus' stony colors camouflage it perfectly in this environment. Caked in mud, algae, and the parasites that grow there, even touching Imperatosuchus' dorsal scales can cause an infection!

name: Indochicyon
description: The black fur of the Indochicyon perfectly represents what it is: a nocturnal killer. Using darkness to conceal itself, it swoops down on its prey at astonishing speed, and before the victim even realizes the danger, its life is brutally taken by the claws of this apex predator!

name: Indolycan
description: Communities on the edges of Indolycan's vast hunting range call it the "Spirit wolf." Swifter than any of her canine ancestors, Indolycan's blue stripes are known to glint in the moonlight just before she attacks.

name: Indominus Rex
description: This intensely aggressive and intelligent hybrid stands 13 feet tall and measures 50 feet in length. It can communicate with raptors and cloak!

name: Indominus Rex Gen 2
description: A dangerous attempt by InGen to alter the already vicious Indominus rex resulted in this GEN 2. The creature’s yellow stripes become brighter with the adrenaline rush she experiences after a successful hunt.

name: Indonemys
description: Like its hybrid ancestor, the scaly Smilonemys, this Apex predator uses the stony texture of its back to camouflage itself within rock formations. Some specialists believe Indonemys may also be able to disguise its heat signature, further confusing prey!

name: Indoraptor
description: One handler called the Indoraptor the "Stealth fighter jet of dinosaurs." This member of the Chimeridae family is extremely intelligent and can echolocate.

name: Indoraptor Gen 2
description: The source of the DNA used to create this GEN 2 is hinted at in her pigmentation. This creature’s light scales are reflective, and are used to blind her prey before devouring them.

name: Indotaurus
description: The fierce Indotaurus is a fusion of Carnotaurus and Indominus rex, two predators who have hunted near the top of the Isla Nublar food chain! Could there be a new Isla Nublar alpha in our midst?

name: Inostherium
description: At about 300 lbs, Inostherium’s jaw makes up for a quarter of its total body weight. Massive muscles run between the creature's mouth and shoulders so its jaw wouldn't hang open at all times.

name: Inostrancevia
description: Inostrancevia is the largest known Gorgonopsidae, an ancient family of saber-toothed predators. This carnivore has highly developed canine teeth, which are put to work while hunting its preferred meal, Scutosaurus. Unfortunately for Inostrancevia, Scutosaurus’ armored body is difficult for even a saber-tooth to penetrate.

name: Irritator
description: The Irritator's name describes the feeling of the paleontologists who had to spend many hours restoring its skull. This pesky creature is actually a relative of the vicious Spinosaurus!

name: Irritator Gen 2
description: This pale Irritator may have developed its unique coloring to camouflage in a grassland environment. More data on the creature's behaviour in the wild is needed to confirm this theory.

name: Junior
description: This Juvenile T. rex is the offspring of the Tyrannosaurs Buck and Doe!

name: Kaprosuchus
description: You might mistake the Kaprosuchus for a 20 foot crocodile except for its long legs. This ancient predator actually spends most of its time on land.

name: Kelenken
description: With a skull measuring more than 2 feet, the Kelenken has the largest head of any known bird. The Kelenken's immense beak has tremendous bite force, and is used as a hunting weapon. As a result, this carnivorous creature was named "Terror Bird".

name: Kentrorex
description: Taking some samples from a unique Kentrosaurus, Mantah Corp figured that even a herbivore could make for a powerful bioengineered weapon with the right experimentation. The Kentrorex is the result: the cranial crest helps protect the only weak spot it used to have with sharp, solid spikes and thick bone plates. A stronger muscular structure and fierce instincts compound to make it a combative herbivore with a thagomizer that can strike with enough force to pierce solid metal.

name: Kentrosaurus
description: The backward-pointing spikes of Kentrosaurus make this dinosaur very difficult to ambush.

name: Keratoporcus
description: Keratoporcus is a nightmarish Cenozoic hybrid that will eat almost anything. Its teeth are double the length of the creatures' it was fused from, making it unable to completely shut its jaw. This condition forces this omnivore to swallow its meals in unchewed chunks.

name: Koolabourgiana
description: This predatory pterosaur hybrid is known to some dinosaur trainers as the 'sky-dog.' Others have compared it to a predatory flying fox! Its short, beakless snout makes it more dangerous in ground-based attacks than dives from the air.

name: Koolasuchus
description: Like some modern species of frog, the Koolasuchus buries deep in mud and hibernates during cool seasons. When awake, this amphibious Brachyopoid likes to skulk at the bottom of shallow pools and wait for prey.

name: Koolasuchus Gen 2
description: Due to its small size, this Koolasuchus clone developed a uniquely intimidating call to ward off predators.

name: Koreanosaurus
description: The Koreanosaurus is a burrowing dinosaur, using its large frontal claws to dig in the ground!

name: Lambeosaurus
description: The Lambeosaurus is a herbivore that lived during the Cretaceous period. It is particularly known for its hollow cranial crest, resembling a small hat. This crest had several functions! It allowed it to regulate its temperature while also enabling it to amplify its roars, appearing more imposing to its predators!

name: Leaellynasaura
description: Living in small social groups, Leaellynasaura likely communicates with chirps and body signals, keeping in touch while foraging for ferns and soft plants.

name: Leucistic Baryonyx
description: Fused from Baryonyx and Mexican tetra DNA, this genetically modified Baryonyx is white, eyeless and has incredibly sensitive senses of hearing and smell!

name: Little Eatie
description: This juvenile tyrannosaurus, named "Little Eatie", is still learning independence and the natural instincts necessary for a tyrannosaurus to survive in the wild. However, it showcases amazing ferocity if aggrieved, and a level of cunning unseen in other tyrannosaurus species when hunting alongside its mother.

name: Lystrosaurus
description: Meet Lystrosaurus, the 'shovel lizard!' This tiny herbivore is a powerful digger that nests with its kin in burrows overnight.

name: Lystrosavis
description: The fiery-plumed Lystrosavis is omnivorous. Its inverted tusks assist this creature in digging for roots as well as rending meals of carrion!

name: Lystrosuchus
description: Mature Lystrosuchus resemble juvenile Ceratopsids, leading some groups of these creatures to even be adopted into Triceratops herds!

name: Lythronax
description: Lythronax evolved before T. rex, and was the largest carnivore of its ecosystem at the time. This gives the 'gore king' a highly aggressive behaviour, often pitting it against other large predators.

name: Magnapyritor
description: Magnapyritor comes from the Latin for 'Great Fire.' This superhybrid fuses the vicious intelligence of the Pyrritator with the sturdy defensive make-up of the Dimetrodon.

name: Magnaraptor
description: Dominance displays among Magnaraptor are among the most complex ever observed by specialists. This highly intelligent superhybrid will engage in threat dances for hours against opponents, communicating information not yet understood by science.

name: Maiasaura
description: The discovery of the Maiasaura among remains of adolescent offspring helped confirm that large herbivorous dinosaurs cared for their young. Since then we have learned this gentle hadrosaur, whose name means 'Good Mother Lizard,' actually hatched and raised its offspring in large nesting colonies. The behavior mirrors that of some modern seabird species.

name: Majundaboa
description: The fusion of pigmentation from two differently colored creatures forms the impressive patterns on Majundaboa’s scales. These patterns help the colossal snake to camouflage within forested environments. Instead of giving chase, Majundaboa will ambush unsuspecting prey.

name: Majundasuchus
description: Spliced with ancient crocodilian DNA, Majundasuchus is one of the fastest and most flexible attackers in the theropod bloodline. Unlike most dinosaurs, it also has impeccable eyesight.

name: Majungasaurus
description: Although it resembles a Tyrannosaurus rex, the Majungasaurus is an entirely separate species of dinosaur. Fossil evidence indicates that this savage predator was so bloodthirsty that it engaged in cannibalism!

name: Mammolania
description: One of the slower Cenozic hybrids, the Mammolania is lucky not to have many predators to outrun. Its skin is tough and scaly, even beneath the patches of fur, and it weighs roughly 6,000 kg, making it both difficult to knock down and capable of toppling most taller opponents.

name: Mammotherium
description: This Cenozoic hybrid’s horn is situated lower than the Elasmotherium’s, allowing it to look forward while charging opponents. Mammotheriums rub their horns together to keep their horns sharp, and to show affection for each other.

name: Marsupial Lion
description: This ancient carnivorous marsupial is most closely related to modern-day kangaroos and koalas, not lions. Its semiopposable first digit and strong limbs make the creature, also known as the Thylacoleo carnifex, an excellent climber. It uses this skill to ambush its prey, leaping from branches onto their backs.

name: Masiakasaurus
description: This vicious lizard has very unique dentition-- forward facing teeth! These were probably used for catching a specialized fish diet.

name: Mastodonsaurus
description: With its massive, flat head and long body, the Mastodonsaurus is perfectly adapted for lurking in murky swamp waters. It ambushes prey like fish and amphibians by snapping its powerful jaws as they swim by.

name: Megalania
description: The Megalania is an extinct species of Monitor lizard, the largest ground lizard to ever exist!

name: Megaloceros
description: This woodlands creature stands slightly taller than a modern moose at the shoulder. With its long, powerful legs, Megaloceros is the most efficient runner in the deer family.

name: Megalocevia
description: As this Apex creature ages, the blue hues on its back and neck become more distinct, indicating brood seniority!

name: Megalogaia
description: Unlike most stegosauridae, this hybrid's plates are not made from hardened scales, but are extensions of the creature's spine. Because Megalogaia plates never stop growing, offspring with underdeveloped back muscles will not survive past adolescence.

name: Megalonyx
description: This giant ground sloth only went extinct about 11,000 years ago! Early humans may have had something to do with it, since cut-marked bones of Megalonyx in Ohio indicate it was hunted by Ohioan Paleo-Americans!

name: Megalosaurus
description: The relative intelligence of this ancient predator makes it an adaptive hunter. Megalosaurus can easily take down both land and water prey!

name: Megalosuchus
description: If Gorgosuchus is a panther, Megalosuchus is a Siberian tiger wrapped in razor wire. This highly intelligent carnivore is lightning-quick and has a bite-force greater than a saltwater crocodile's. It has also been observed climbing trees and sheer rock faces aided by its powerful foreclaws.

name: Megalotops
description: Unlike other giant ground sloths, Megalotops lives alone in deeply wooded areas. Unique species of moss and algae grow in the under-fur of this mysterious creature, which blossoms into beautiful flowers once their seeds reach the forest floor.

name: Megamimus
description: Megamimus inherits the speed and agility of its ancestor Procerathomimus, making it a powerful predator in its own right. Using this agility, it can draw in prey attempting to strike and flee only to inflict grievous wounds in turn.

name: Megaraptor
description: The megaraptor seems to exhibit a social structure comparable to lions and hyenas. Due to their large size, they can more easily confront prey than other raptors who often must use pack tactics to do so instead.

name: Megatyrannus
description: This colorful hybrid's physical features have been reduced in size, but not diminished in efficiency. Its nasal crest is  allows it to defend itself from a position of weakness while the dorsal fin allows it agile movement when hunting. Its highly visible plumage serves as a warning to other predators, as approaching a Megatyrannus without proper precautions is extremely dangerous.

name: Megistocurus
description: The hybrid Megistocurus is more playful and nurturing than its hybrid parent Megistotherium. This creature has even been observed by specialists rolling around in playful 'somersault' behaviour with other pack members!

name: Megistotherium
description: Watch out for Megistotherium! This carnivore was one of the most tenacious predators to inhabit Africa during the Miocene. Its size rivals the modern Grizzly Bear, and its teeth marks have been found defacing Mastodon fossils, proving that size did not matter when it came to hunting.

name: Meiolania
description: This ancient stem-turtle is thought to be a very distant member of the ankylosauridae family. The horns extending from its flat skull, and its armored tail, support this suggestion.

name: Microraptor
description: The tiny dromaeosaurid Microraptor sports a wing on each of its 4 limbs. The unique adaptation assists this prehistoric biplane in powered flight and gliding between treetops!

name: Miragaia
description: In Greek myth, Gaia is the personification of the Earth and the mother of the gods of Olympus. Miragaia translates to 'wonderful Earth.'  The name suits this gentle herbivore!

name: Monkeydactyl
description: The Monkeydactyl is the only known pterosaur with opposable thumbs, making it a fantastic climber! These nimble flyers spend their days swooping between trees and gripping branches, using their claws to pluck insects or small fruits from high up.

name: Monkeyondactylus
description: Monkeyondactylus' grasping hands allow it to use hard surfaces to slingshot itself to high speeds despite its armor plates. Their swarming movements confuse both prey and predators alike, exhausting them without much fatigue for the flock.

name: Monolometrodon
description: Monolometrodon has been observed performing significantly more complex display rituals than its sailed ancestor, Dimetrodon. This may be because the Monolophosaurus, Monolometrodon's other ancestor, evolved later than Dimetrodon and could have developed more nuanced social instincts. Further research is necessary.

name: Monolomoth
description: The bulky Monolomoth can reach impressive running speeds of up to 40 milies per hour, on both snow and plains!

name: Monolophosaurus
description: The crest on the skull of Monolophosaurus isn't for combat. It's a display structure meant to attract members of its own species.

name: Monolophosaurus Gen 2
description: The genome of this mottled blue theropod is so resilient that Monolophosaurus GEN 2 is now more abundant in the wild than InGen's original!

name: Monolorhino
description: This omnivorous superhybrid can modify its core temperature by decreasing the blood circulation in the sail on its back. When blood circulation in the sail drops, the creature’s core temperature rises and the sail becomes a deeper shade of violet.

name: Monomimus
description: This swift hybrid is more aggressive than the Gallimimus it resembles. It can use its impressive cranial crest for communication and intimidation in battle.

name: Monostegotops
description: This fusion of herbivore and predator genotypes is more aggressive than its purely herbivore relatives. Monostegotops uses its strong hind legs and horned shield to charge and pin enemies.

name: Moros Intrepidus
description: First discovered in 2013, this tiny tyrannosaurid dates to the Cretaceous period and weighs roughly 170 lbs!

name: Mortem Rex
description: InGEN denies any part in the creation of this modified Tyrannosaurus rex, but admit to tracking its Map movements. Their specialists have noticed a pattern of unique territorial behavior. Strangely, this pattern sometimes includes the help of other creatures.

name: Moschops
description: A thick skull and heavy jaw forced Moschops to develop strong jaw muscles, which makes chewing through tough vegetation possible. This herbivore’s elbows joints are shaped similarly to mammals, allowing Moschops to better support its enormous body, and even raise itself on its hind legs for short periods of time.

name: Mutadon
description: The savage and relentless hunter Mutadon is a mutant fusion of Velociraptor and Pteranodon DNA! It was first discovered on the abandoned research facility on Ile St. Hubert!

name: Nanophosaurus
description: The Nanophosaurus' spinal fin may seem like an unwanted evolution at first glance, but it is covered in spines of considerable size, allowing it to fend off attackers passively. Its jaw retains its crushing force, only with more dense teeth enhancing its already powerful bite.

name: Nanotyrannus
description: While initially thought to be a juvenile Tyrannosaurus, this specific dinosaur is not the same species as the large predator. Its jaws are just as powerful though, able to inflict severe wounds even to same size prey.

name: Nasutoceratops
description: This ceratopsid dinosaur is related to the famous Triceratops. Unlike the Triceratops, Nasutoceratops' horns protrude straight out from its skull and reach almost to the tip of its nose!

name: Nodopatosaurus
description: The wide feet of this hybrid allow it to tread looser turf than other Apatosaurs. This helps it get to food others can't reach, an unexpected specialization caused by its hybridization.

name: Nodopatotitan
description: The gigantic body of the Nodopatotitan has some unique traits. The longer, stronger tail granted by its Apatosaur ancestry gives it impeccable balance and surprising speed for a dinosaur so large.

name: Nodosaurus
description: Unlike other Ankylosaurs, the Nodosaurus has no club on its powerful tail. But that doesn't make this herbivore's attacks any less damaging!

name: Nomingia
description: Nomingia may be a small Oviraptor, but it has a lot of personality! The crest and unique feather fan of the Nomingia are used for mating displays.

name: Nominrex
description: This Apex Terror Bird is recognizable by its screeching nocturnal calls, which are thought to be used to mark territory. Listen for it at night!

name: Nundasuchus
description: Built like a crocodile with longer legs, the Nundasuchus can run down prey on land or drag it into the water. In battle, it uses its powerful jaws more than its tail or claws.

name: Nyctopteryx
description: This hybrid inherits the two main characteristics of its parents: The antler-like horn and sharp teeth. The horn has an interesting property - its bright-red coloration is so unnatural to other predators that they often get distracted by it, allowing the Nyctopteryx to flee.

name: Nyctosaurus
description: The 'night lizard' Nyctosaurus is impossible to mistake among other pterosaurs due to its large, antler-like cranial crest. It is likely this crest is used in displays to other members of its species, just like modern deer brandish their antlers!

name: Olorotitan
description: The duck-billed Olorotitan is a gentle giant. This hadrosaur stood up to 12 feet tall and weighed up to 3.5 metric tons!

name: Ophiacodon
description: The massive jaws of the Ophiacodon are deceptively precise. This small predator expertly targets weak areas in its prey's defenses to more easily bring them down to its level.

name: Ornithomimus
description: Though feathered, the Ornithomimus is flightless. Like a modern day ostrich, it instead uses its long legs to run at high speeds!

name: Ouranosaurus
description: The Ouranosaurus is a social dinosaur. Some paleontologists believe this hadrosaur's sail was important for socialization, though its full function is still under debate.

name: Ovilophomoloch
description: The fancifully feathered Ovilophomoloch does not headbutt for dominance like its hybrid parent Stygimoloch GEN 2. Instead, this vibrantly colored creature competes in synchronized dancing displays! That means the larger and brighter an Ovilophomoloch's feathers, the more authority it has within its brood!

name: Ovilophosaurus
description: This hybrid predator has stereoscopic vision, which allows it gauge the distance between points better than most other creatures.

name: Ovinnolophosaurus
description: This flame-coloured  hybrid is both swift and elusive! When seen, it is taken as a symbol of luck by specialists!

name: Oviraptor
description: Oviraptor means “Egg Thief” in Latin. The first remains from this small predator were discovered on top of what appeared to be ceratopsian eggs. After the creature was named the eggs were proven to be from a theropod, but the name stuck regardless.

name: Oviraptor Gen 2
description: The Epic Oviraptor GEN 2 bears a unique white coloration across its feathers! Specialists believe this may help it camouflage in sand and surf environments.

name: Ovylenken
description: With a strong preference for the eggs of other dinosaurs, the Ovylenken, thanks to its crossbreeding with a Gryposuchus, can widen its beak significantly to swallow them whole! Beware of its greed; be cautious not to leave your eggs unattented!

name: Pachycephalosaurus
description: The dome atop the skull of Pachycephalosaurus can be as much as 10 inches thick! While generally not aggressive to other species, these herbivores often tussle with each other.

name: Panthera
description: One of four lethal atrociraptors bred to kill on command, Panthera is swift enough to pursue a speeding motorcycle across long distances-- and deadly enough to take out whoever may be riding it!

name: Panthera Blytheae
description: This ancestor of the modern snow leopard lived in the mountains of Tibet roughly 6 million years ago! Cold-adaptations of this predator and its megafaunal prey perfectly suitied the later Pleistocene Ice Age, making Tibet a cradle of Pleistocene megafauna!

name: Pantherator
description: Little is known of the solitary Pantherator. These hunters prefer to dwell in only the highest alpine regions, creating territories of solitude that even Panthera Blythea will not challenge.

name: Paramoloch
description: This fusion of the Parasaurolophus and Stygimoloch has a unique cranial crest that allows it to both headbutt and communicate with other creatures.

name: Parasaurolophus
description: Parasaurolophus is able to use its crest as a resonating chamber to make sounds akin to a foghorn.

name: Parasaurolophus Lux
description: The epithet “lux”, meaning light, was given to creatures who carry the luminosity trait. This trait can be difficult to detect from DNA samples, and so the most common way to distinguish a Parasaurolophus lux is to observe it at night.

name: Parasauthops
description: Like that of its hybrid parent the Parasaurolophus lux, the cause of Parasauthops' bioluminescence is a mystery. Could it be for camouflage, attraction, or conceal some other quality? Fusion of more of these glowing creatures will help find an answer...

name: Phorurex
description: Phorurex literally means "carrier king." Specialists have taken to nicknaming this armored Phorusaur something simpler: 'the Gryphon.' Both titles suit the territorial superhybrid, which carries its armor with it everywhere in the bony scutes around its skull and thick, lustrous feathers!

name: Phorusaura
description: Unlike its nurturing relative Maiasaura, this vicious bird would attempt to destroy the offspring of other Phorusaura to reduce competition within the flock.

name: Phorusrhacos
description: Phorusrhacos is a type of 'terror bird' that lived during the Miocene. This predator stalked the grasslands of current day Patagonia, and stood up to 8 feet (2.5 meters) tall!

name: Pierce
description: Pierce is a Kentrosaurus that was raised alongside a smilodon, allowing Pierce to form social connections even with other species! Its tail is exceptionally powerful even for a Kentrosaurus, making it a ferocious combatant despite its herbivore diet.

name: Plateopikasaurus
description: Although the behaviour has not yet been observed, many experts believe the foreclaws of the Plateopikasaurus may be opposable, allowing it to grasp and manipulate objects!

name: Plateorex
description: The striking patterns on the hide of the Plateorex are believed to help in camouflage in arid grassy and plains environments, not unlike some modern antelope!

name: Plateosaurus
description: This so called 'prosauropod' was a bipedal herbivore resembling an early form of later quadrupedal sauropods!

name: Postimetrodon
description: The sail of the Dimetrodon makes this long-legged crocodilian more conspicuous than the Postosuchus. The creature has been known to cache its prey underwater for later feeding.

name: Postosuchus
