description: Postosuchus can switch between bipedal and quadrupedal locomotion, like a bear. This ancestor of the crocodile was an apex predator of its time.

name: Poukaidei
description: This hybrid has claws on its wings, making it an even more effective bird of prey than the Haast Eagle. Poukaidei is also larger than the Haast Eagle, and can carry prey weighing up to 130 lbs in its talons.

name: Poukandactylus
description: Poukandactylus’ fantastic feathers, chaotic colors, and powerful patterns make demanding attention an easy task. Its loud appearance triggers frequent duels with other predatory creatures of the sky.

name: Preondactylus
description: Like modern day gulls, Preondactylus congregate around shorelines to feed on fish, molluscs, and insects. Keep your shoreline clean to avoid damaging this tiny flyer's new habitat!

name: Procerathomimus
description: Procerathomimus is swift and nimble. Its green hue helps it camouflage in lush environments, while the strong legs it inherited from the Proceratosaurus make it an excellent sprinter.

name: Proceratosaurus
description: While it may look meek, this small but savage predator is actually one of the earliest known Tyrannosaurs.

name: Proto Lux
description: Proto Lux are believed to be able to walk bipedally for short periods, using their clubbed tails as ballast.

name: Protoceratops
description: Though small, the Protoceratops is not short on techniques to escape the most perilous situations! By waving its tail adorned with long hairs, it can distract its predator, allowing it to prepare its escape!

name: Protonodon
description: Like the porcupine, the Protonodon has protruding spines on its back! By doing so, predators think twice before making it their next meal. Those who mess with it, get pricked!

name: Pteranodon
description: Pteranodon tend to roost in groups, often near cliffsides where updrafts are common. In battle, their darting speed make them crafty opponents capable of multiple swooping style attacks.

name: Pteranokyrie
description: Named after the Valkyrie of Norse myth, Pteranokyrie means "winged chooser." Watch out for this Apex flyer slicing across the skies-- it could be a harbinger of your end!

name: Pteraquetzal
description: The long distance gliding speed of the massive Pteraquetzal is unrivaled. This allows the pterosaur to take long flights across the ocean in search of food and nesting grounds.

name: Pterovexus
description: This pterosaur superhybrid got the name 'vex' for its mischievious nature. Its high intelligence and speed make it hard to catch, and even harder to keep contained!

name: Pulmonoscorpius
description: This ancient scorpion lives in the carboniferous period. Some fossils suggest it could grow to more than 2 feet in length!

name: Purrolyth
description: If you've ever had nightmares of crocodiles that could run on two legs, they just came true. This hybrid brings all the strength and aggressiveness of the Purussaurus to a speedy theropod frame.

name: Purussaurus
description: This prehistoric reptile is actually a type of caiman!

name: Purussaurus Gen 2
description: The unique coloring of the Purussaurus GEN 2 is probably a result of regional adaptations. Like most GEN 2 creatures, this reptile would have needed to camouflage in different environments than its relative.

name: Purutaurus
description: This fusion of Carnotaurus and Purussaurus DNA is a very dangerous hybrid. The compact, intelligent theropod matches its speed with highly dexterous fore-limbs and excellent predatory instincts.

name: Pyroraptor
description: Engineered by Biosyn, the Pyroraptor is a terrifying fire-red feathered dinosaur with enlarged curved claws. Its stealth movements provide this deadly predator the ability to hunt its target even from underneath icy waters of a frozen lake.

name: Pyroraptor Gen 2
description: The feathered Pyroraptor GEN 2 may be more colorful than the Velociraptor, but is just as dangerous. This dinosaur is also an excellent tree-climber.

name: Pyrorixis
description: Pyrorixis means fire-breaker." It's a fitting name for this wild Atrociraptor hybrid, which has been observed ramming prey with its head down, shattering bones before attacking with teeth and claws!

name: Pyrosuchus
description: This bulky pyroraptor hybrid is adapted for hunting in both land and aquatic environments, and can even keep pace with the more slender Pyroraptor on the run!

name: Pyrritator
description: The sleek and vicous Pyrritator combines the intelligence of the Pyroraptor with the aggressiveness of the Spinosaur order.

name: Quetzalcoatlus
description: Engineered by Biosyn, this Late Cretaceous period pterosaur is one of the largest animals to ever fly. Named after the Aztec feathered serpent god, Quetzalcoatlus commands the skies over Biosyn Valley and is capable of flying around the world!

name: Quetzalcoatlus Gen 2
description: The Epic Quetzalcoatlus GEN 2 spreads its wings a whopping 36 feet! By catching the right air currents, this toothless pterosaur is capable of flying across continents, or even around the world!

name: Quetzaljara
description: The brilliantly colored Quetzaljara grows slightly larger than either of its hybrid ancestors. Its dynamic wing shape and aerodynamic crest also make it more agile in the air than Tapejara!

name: Quetzorion
description: This fusion of theropod and pterosaur bloodlines is known to be incredibly aggressive, and loves to eat anything it can get its beak around.

name: Rajadorixis
description: Don't be fooled by its snow-white color! The Rajadorixis is only waiting for one thing: for you to lower your guard so it can make you its next meal!

name: Rajadotholus
description: Combining a Rajatholus with a Scelidosaurus was an extremely good idea, as this new species combines the aggressiveness of the former while granting it the rough and protective skin of the latter! A beautiful scientific achievement!

name: Rajakylosaurus
description: Heavily armored predators are rare in the natural world because the weight of their protection slows them down. Instead of chasing down prey, Rajakylosaurus uses its bulk to muscle other predators off of fresh kills.

name: Rajasaurus
description: Like its relative Carnotaurus, the distinctive bumps on the Rajasaurus's skull help this theropod identify other members of its species at a distance.

name: Rajatholus
description: The combination of a Rajasaurus and a Sphaerotholus led to the emergence of this new species! It combines the ferocity of the former with the distinctive skull of the latter. A terrible creature indeed!

name: Rativates
description: Rativates means 'raft seer'-- so named for its resemblance to the extant bird species ratites. Rativates is a swift moving ornithomimid that stood smaller compared to most of its cousins.

name: Rebel
description: Contrary to his name, Rebel is sweet and very affectionate! He loves to play with the humans who come to see him! Especially since he's very fond of food and will never refuse a little snack! Just so you know!

name: Rebirth Raptor
description: Ile Saint-Hubert has allowed us to observe behavioral differences between various species of dinosaur, and this velociraptor is no exception. Its fight or flight instinct is overdeveloped to a point where it often hurts itself fighting, but its cunning allows it to pick its battles and escape if necessary.

name: Rebirth Spinosaurus
description: This InGen created variant of the Spinosaurus was first spotted on Ile St. Hubert hunting alongside the aquatic dinosaur Mosasaurus, displaying a never-before observed mutualism!

name: Rebirth Spinosaurus Gen 2
description: Like the other Spinosaurus specimen found on Ile Saint-Hubert, this dinosaur shows the ability to cooperate with other species to hunt, moving out of danger as necessary.

name: Rebirth T. Rex
description: This Ile Saint-Hubert variant of the Tyrannosaurus Rex shows substantial cunning when hunting for prey. Its instincts are honed to perfection, allowing for bite attacks in the weak parts of prey's defenses.

name: Red
description: Named for her unique coppery-red scales, Red is one of four lethal Atrociraptors engineered for viciousness.

name: Refrenantem
description: Refrenantem translates to 'rush' in Latin, which holds 2 meanings. The first is a nod to its wetland habitat, where it can be found snaking through bulrushes. The second heeds warning: this creature's attacks are explosive, and in the time it takes its prey to realize danger the battle will already be lost.

name: Rexy
description: After the closure of Jurassic Park, Rexy reigned supreme over Isla Nublar. Captured and used as an attraction in Jurassic World, this powerful predator escaped again to tangle for dominance with the Indominus rex!

name: Rinchenia
description: Rinchenia had an agile frame equipped for stalking prey, and a crest on its skull that might have been used to attract mates.

name: Rinchicyon
description: Ever heard of a wolf in sheep's clothing? The tricky predatory Rinchicyon uses its resemblance to its hybrid parent Rinchenia to infiltrate their flocks and prey upon weaker specimens! Fortunately Rinchenia can still outrun this dastardly hybrid!

name: Rodrigues Solitaire
description: Closely related to the Dodo, the Rodrigues solitaire is native to the island of Rodrigues, near Madagascar. Like the Dodo, this flightless bird went extinct in the 18th century due to colonial extermination.

name: Sah Panthera
description: Sah' is the Tibetan word for the modern snow leopard. However, Sah Panthera more resembles the mythical Snow Lion of Buddhist tradition.

name: Sarcomantarx
description: Known as the "Ironclad Stalker" in its ecosystem, Sarcomantarx uses its camouflage to blend into the rocky, iron-rich terrain of its home, waiting patiently for the perfect moment to strike.

name: Sarcorixis
description: The Sarcorixis is as vicious as a desert scorpion. Its spike-covered body makes it dangerous from every angle, and its jaws lock with a strength greater than any living crocodile.

name: Sarcosaurus
description: The Sarcosaurus, whose name means 'Meat lizard', embodies the natural evolution towards the large carnivores of the Cretaceous period. Larger than its ancestors, it already possesses all the attributes of the perfect predator, with its jaw filled with sharp teeth and its muscular hind legs that enable it to swiftly pounce on its prey.

name: Sarcosuchus
description: This killer reptile grows up to 40 feet long. It drags large prey underwater, then caches its kills in the soft river mud.

name: Saurolyth
description: Saurolyth uses its iridescent feathers to communicate: shifting its posture and fluffing out its plumage to signal dominance, warnings, or cooperative intent.

name: Saurophaganax
description: Saurophaganax means 'lord of lizard eaters!' The largest terrestrial carnivore of Late Jurassic North America, it weighs in at a whopping 3.8 metric tons!

name: Saurornitholestes
description: Unlike its larger raptor cousins, this swift hunter relies primarily on its speed to both hunt and evade opponents, able to quickly weave through dense foliage with near-silent footsteps!

name: Scaphognathus
description: Scaphognathus is physically similar to another small pterosaur: Rhamphorhynchus. However, many experts believe that while the prehistoric Rhamphorhynchus was nocturnal, Scaphognathus was most active during the day. This allowed it to exploit different food sources than other pterosaurs its size. More research is required to determine whether Jurassic World's clones share this distinction.

name: Scaphotator
description: The debate is still open on whether the Scaphotator is an insectivore or a carnivore. There is no proof yet of this bizarre superhybrid eating anything other than insects, yet the structure of its teeth and strength of its jaw suggest a meat diet.

name: Scelidosaurus
description: This armoured ornithiscian is a native of the modern day British Isles!  Related to ankylosaurs and stegosaurs, Scelidosaurus' armour resembles the bony scutes of today's crocodiles and armadillos.

name: Scolosaurus
description: Scolosaurus is an ankylosaurid that lived around 76.5 million years ago in the semi-arid, seasonal climate of modern-day Alberta, Canada. At that time, volcanoes periodically blanketed the region in ash, helping to create an excellent repository of fossils known as the Dinosaur Park Formation!

name: Scorpios Rex
description: This vicious hybrid takes the name 'Scorpios' from the venomous quills she sports on her tail. The Scorpios rex can eject these quills at will, spraying them in all directions when she goes in for the attack!

name: Scorpios Rex Gen 2
description: Little is known about the full capabilities of the Scorpios rex GEN 2 or the powers of its venom. Records on the hybrid's creation have been bizarrely erased... Whatever it was, the genetic variations seem to have also given this creature its eerie grey hue...

name: Scorpios Rex Gen 3
description: Almost nothing is known of the mysterious Scorpios rex GEN 3 or the initial genetic processes used to create her. It is said that, like the original Scorpios rex, this unique hybrid is drawn to bright light and heat... sometimes skulking in the shadows just beyond the light's limits.

name: Scutophicyon
description: The powerful forelegs of this hybrid make it a swift runner with a very unique set of tracks! These forelegs also make Scutophicyon a great digger, a skill that comes in handy when burrowing after evasive prey or fashioning dens for overnight lodging.

name: Scutosaurus
description: The combination of short legs and heavy weight makes Scutosaurus quite slow, but being unable to flee predators is not necessarily a problem for this herbivore, thanks to its nearly impenetrable bony armor.

name: Secodontosaurus
description: While its jaws are most specialized for snapping up fish, the amphibious Secodontosaurus can also use its slender snout for precise wounding attacks on larger predators.

name: Segnoraptor
description: These large terrestrial hybrids have seasonal flocking behaviors much like modern ostriches! Depending on the time of year, you can find them alone, in small groups, or in groups of up to 100!

name: Segnosaurus
description: This therizinosaur lived in Mongolia during the Late Cretaceous. Slow moving, they use their long claws for foraging and defense. Young Segnosauri can even use them to climb trees!

name: Segnotherisaurus
description: The heavy down of this superhybrid helps it adapt to cold environments, where it uses its long claws to fish in icewater.

name: Shantungosaurus
description: Shantungosaurus is the largest hadrosaur ever found, weighing in at an estimated 13 to 16 tons! That size alone is enough to deter smaller predators as their meager defenses get crushed when trying to feast upon the Shantungosaurus.

name: Shantunrax
description: A mixture of the living citadel, Animantarx, and the largest hadrosaur ever found, the Shantunrax is just as large as its ancestor while adding dorsal plates with spines to ensure predators think twice about feeding on it. The real surprise is how agile it is, as the Shantunrax can reposition itself and use its considerable weight and spikes to fend off attackers.

name: Sinoceratops
description: Sinoceratops are herbivores. However, some juveniles have been known to eat meat to help fuel their rapid skeletal growth.

name: Sinokotaraptor
description: The Sinokotaraptor is an excellent climber. Though far too heavy for flight, the feathers of the Sinokotaraptor's forelimbs help it keep its balance when pinning down prey in the trees!

name: Sinosauropteryx
description: The ring-tailed Sinosauropteryx may seem furred, but its skin is actually covered in downy, filament-like feathers. In fact, Sinosauropteryx was the first dinosaur to be discovered sporting feather-like structures all over its body, helping to prove dinosaurs evolved into modern birds!

name: Sinraptor
description: Despite its name, Sinraptor is not a dromaeosaurid, but a member of the family Metriacanthosauridae.

name: Skoolasaurus
description: Skoolasaurus bears the body shape of the Koolasuchus but the heavy armor of its other progenitor, Scolosaurus. This creature likes to make its home by burrowing deep into soft dirt and mud, lining the entrance with rocks that help it camouflage.

name: Skoonametrodon
description: Although powerful with teeth and claws, Skoonametrodon has an extra advantage. Each scale of this Apex theropod comes to a jagged point, allowing it to rake against any creature it comes into contact with like a cheese-grater!

name: Skoonasaurus
description: Skoonasaurus are most commonly found wading in fast moving rivers. The first scientist to observe this behavior remarked that the creature looked like a schooner. How appropriate!

name: Skorpiodactylus
description: Combining the ferocity of the Skorpiovenator with the agility of the Dimodactylus, the Skorpiodactylus is much more dangerous than its two parents! It is capable of swooping down on its prey at an immense speed and tearing it apart before it can react!

name: Skorpiostegotops
description: The Skorpiostegotops is a marvel of natural defense! Between its dorsal spines as hard as stone, where predators come to break their teeth, and the spikes on its tail that it doesn't hesitate to use to strike its opponents, nothing seems to be able to stop it!

name: Skorpiovenator
description: Contrary to its cousins, the Skorpiovenator possesses a thinner and elongated head, which allows it, during attacks, to penetrate deeper into the flesh of its victim, inflicting even more damage!

name: Smilocephalosaurus
description: This fusion of the Smilodon and Pachycephalosaurus has been known to butt heads with members of its family group, just like its famous hybrid ancestor.

name: Smilodon
description: Popularly known as the Saber-Toothed Tiger, Smilodon lived in the Eocene and Pleistocene epochs. This precision hunter preyed on large herbivores like bison and went extinct only 10,000 years ago.

name: Smilonemys
description: This unusual Superhybrid uses its armored back to disguise itself amongst rocky landscapes. When prey is near, the Smilonemys pounces and then strikes with its large fangs.

name: Sonorasaurus
description: At 29 feet, this Sauropod was taller than Apatosaurus, but shorter than Brachiosaurus. Sonorasaurus was a Cretaceous dinosaur, and the discovery of its fossils proved that Sauropods survived beyond the Jurassic period.

name: Sphaerotholus
description: Sphaerotholus is a genus of dinosaurs that lived during the Late Cretaceous period. To date, five species have been discovered, all of which lived on the present-day North American continent! They are particularly known for their incredible dome-shaped skulls!

name: Spinoceratops
description: The peaceful Spinoceratops is actually an omnivore, feeding on plants as well as fish, just like the piscivorous Spinosaurus!

name: Spinoconstrictor
description: This gigantic superhybrid hides in tropical river beds by burrowing into the mud with its sharp spinal fins. The Spinoconstrictor hunts by dragging larger prey into the mud to suffocate and be consumed.

name: Spinonyx
description: This fusion of the Baryonyx GEN 2 and the Spinosaurus GEN 2 developed unique mottled red and purple coloring that darkens as it matures. With thicker skin and a more powerful set of jaws than either of its hybrid parents, the Spinonyx is a very dangerous Spinosaur!

name: Spinosaurus
description: Spinosaurus hunts like today's grizzly bears, wading into the water and using its claws to catch slippery fish.

name: Spinosaurus Aegyptiacus
description: Discovered in Egypt's Western desert, the 'Egyptian spine lizard' is the longest known terrestrial carnivore, measuring 14 meters (46 ft) in length!

name: Spinosaurus Gen 2
description: The bones of the semi-aquatic Spinosaur are more dense than most dinosaurs. This helps them achieve neutral buoyancy while fishing.

name: Spinotahraptor
description: The Spinotahraptor is more narrowly built than other raptors, making it one of the fastest predators on the scene. Its highly flexible fore-claws and low build also make it an excellent fisherman, which is unique in the raptor bloodline!

name: Spinotasuchus
description: This fusion of the Spinotahraptor and Kaprosuchus is a highly intelligent fisherman! Its genetic enhancement with water-dwelling creatures makes this dinosaur very comfortable hunting in marshes and pools.

name: Spinotops
description: This compact armored ceratopsian is a surprisingly playful creature, often seen nudging rocks or using its horns to knock over small trees just for fun!

name: Stegoceras
description: Juvenile Stegoceras are called Ornatotholus. Experts use this term when identifying the genus' flat and underdeveloped juvenile skulls. A fully grown Stegoceras skull is round and can reach up to 3 inches at its thickest point.

name: Stegoceratops
description: Equipped with horns, plates, and an impressive shield, this hybrid is a true defensive powerhouse. Its heavy armor can distract and intimidate even the most savage predators.

name: Stegodeus
description: The most powerful of all Stegosaurs, this Superhybrid's name means roofed god.

name: Stegosaurus
description: This 30-foot quadruped is most famous for its iconic plates and friendly nature.

name: Stegosaurus Ungulatus
description: The gentle Stegosaurus ungulatus has longer hind-limbs and smaller dorsal plates than the Common Stegosaurus. The most remarkable difference between these two creatures is the small plates that grow on the neck and head of S. ungulatus.

name: Stegouros
description: This distinctive ankylosaur first discovered in Chile is remarkable both for its small size and the flat shape of the osteoderms on its tail!

name: Struthiomimus
description: "Mimus" means "mimic" in the dinosaur names Struthiomimus, Ornithomimus and Gallimimus. Struthiomimus' incredibly powerful hind limbs and large three-pronged feet make it seem like a devilish imitation of the modern-day Ostrich.

name: Stygidaryx
description: In the wild, Stygidaryx can be observed engaging in aerial headbutt duels high above the treetops! The weighted skulls and tomahawk shaped bodies of these skyborne brawlers also help them deliver brutal hits on ground-level adversaries.

name: Stygimoloch
description: While their dome heads offer a defensive weapon, the Stygimoloch is an unusually wary dinosaur. Most of their aggressive interactions are with each other.

name: Stygimoloch Gen 2
description: Both GENs of the Stygimoloch communicate primarily through snorts and grunts, like today's antelope.

name: Styracosaurus Lux
description: The bioluminescence of the Styracosaurus lux's crest is caused by bony tubercles of the skull that shine through the skin! It is believed that, like some modern chameleons, this light effect is used for communication in the darkness among members of the species.

name: Suchomimus
description: Small pits along the snout of Suchomimus allow it to detect the movement of fish and other prey in the water.

name: Suchotator
description: The Suchotator is a Spinosaur hybrid. The added bulk on this creature gives it a spiky defense against any attacker.

name: Tanycolagreus
description: With a similar intelligence to the Velociraptor, the 11-foot long Tanycolagreus is a skilled tracker. While velociraptors hunt in wolf-like packs, this solo hunter could be better compared to a coyote.

name: Tapejara
description: Tapejara means 'lord of the ways.' Most paleontologists believe Tapejara was most active during the day time. The dark hue discovered when cloning this pterosaur suggests it may be camouflaged for night-time hunting!

name: Tarbognathus
description: Each flock of Tarbognathus, no matter its size, will sport only one specimen with an orange-scaled cranium. The development indicates this particular creature is the flock's Alpha.

name: Tarbosaurus
description: Like a pride male lion, the Tarbosaurus becomes increasingly aggressive as it grows older and larger.

name: Tenontorex
description: The crest on the Tenontorex's head comes from the Dilophosaurus DNA in its ancestor Tyrannolophosaurus. This hybrid is an herbivore and is classified as a hadrosaurid, just like the Tenontosaurus.

name: Tenontosaurus
description: Think of Tenontosaurus like a Jurassic kangaroo! The large, flexible tail of this member of the Iguanodontia family gives it extra balance while walking or fighting on two legs.

name: Testacornibus
description: Ceratopsidae, turtle, and deer DNA were fused to create this multifaceted Cenozoic hybrid. Testacornibus has a pointed mouth and armor covering its mammalian torso. These attributes make it more resistant to predator attacks and able to deal harmful defensive bites.

name: Therizinosaurus
description: The Biosyn Engineered Therizinosaurus sports a twenty-foot-long neck and weighs up to 11,000 pounds. The three razor sharp claws on each of this herbivore's forelimbs measure a whopping three feet, the longest of any known animal!

name: Thoradolosaur
description: This emerald green superhybrid is a force to be reckoned with. The long arms of the Thoradolosaur are more functional than the arms of other tyrannosaurids, allowing it to grab hold of its prey using its claws.

name: Thylaconyx
description: The dorsal stripes and sandy tone of this mammalian-reptile hybrid make it excellently camouflaged for its preferred grassland habitats.

name: Thylacosmilus
description: Thylacosmilus isn't a feline at all—it’s a metatherian, more closely related to modern marsupials! Unlike a Smilodon, Thylacosmilus' teeth never stop growing, and special bony flanges on its lower jaw help protect them from breaking.

name: Thylacotator
description: A fierce fusion of carnivorous DNA has produced a hybrid hunting expert. The colorful sails running down the mammal’s spine are said to make the Thylacotator easier for its prey to spot, which may be the only reason the otherwise unstoppable predator doesn’t overhunt its territory.

name: Thylos Intrepidus
description: Nicknamed 'the Tasmanian tiger of Jurassic World,' this unique superhybrid is one of Alive's few monotremes, or egg-laying mammals! It is believed this is a result of its fusion of mammal and reptile DNA.

name: Tiger
description: One of four lethal Atrociraptors designed to kill, Tiger is a skilled hunter capable of pursuing its prey even across rooftops and densely packed urban environments!

name: Titanoboa
description: The Titanoboa is the largest known snake, weighing 2,500 lbs and reaching a length of 42 feet. This massive creature thrived in the tropical rainforests of the late Paleocene and was mostly piscivorous...with an occasional taste for crocodile.

name: Titanoboa Gen 2
description: The lack of pigment in this enormous Gen 2 makes camouflage difficult, but spotting this snake does not mean escaping it. The Titanoboa Gen 2’s spine is remarkably flexible, and allows it to swim faster than most of its aquatic prey.

name: Titanosaurus
description: Meaning simply 'Titanic Lizard,' the Titanosaurus is a gentle giant sauropod from the Upper Cretaceous!

name: Titanotarkus
description: Along Titanotarkus' tail, osteoderms form flexible but durable ridges. This allows this hybrid to deliver sweeping tail strikes that combine the brute force of a sauropod whip with the club-like shock of a stegosaurs’ defense—each strike capable of toppling even mid-sized carnivores.

name: Titanotholus
description: Titanotholus not only uses its cranial dome for headbutt attacks, but to amplify low-frequency rumbles through the dome's resonant capacity. This can signal herd movements or warn of predators over long distances!

name: Titanotor
description: This fusion of Titanosaurus and Giganotosaurus DNA is one of few carnivorous sauropod hybrids! Like modern giraffes, this dinosaur has been observed challenging others of its species in neck clashes!

name: Toro
description: Toro is a highly aggressive Carnotaurus identical to other members of his species, except for his stunted right horn and the scar on his snout!

name: Tragodistis
description: Like some modern birds, every infant Tragodistis has a unique call that distinguishes it from all others of its species. This is helpful, considering the large groups this dinosaur naturally nests in!

name: Triceratops
description: Despite being a herbivore, the Triceratops is quite aggressive. These Ceratopsians frequently lock horns, leaving visible scratches and lesions on the sides of their bony frills.

name: Triceratops Gen 2
description: Both genetic lines of Triceratops can communicate freely with members of their species. They use a range of expressive bellows and snorts.

name: Troodoboa
description: The feathered serpent Troodoboa is most active as a hunter during the rainy season. This may be one reason for its green coloration: camouflaging the creature against murky water and wet earth. Despite their brilliant color, it is the cranial feathers of the Troodoboa that have earned the creature its nickname: 'sky snake!'

name: Troodon
description: The name Troodon, meaning “wounding tooth”, was given to this dinosaur after a single carnivorous tooth was used to identify its genus.

name: Trykosaurus
description: Trykosaurus has the raw predatory power of the T. rex and the defensive capabilities of the Ankylosaurus. It's also more agile than either, since it is able to use its club tail as a ballast when running at top speed.

name: Trykovenator
description: A long tail adorned with spines, inherited from the stegosaurs, allows Trykovenator to defend itself as much as to attack its prey. Therefore, he's among the most dangerous predators of its generation!

name: Tryostronix
description: An expert at disabling the defenses of its prey with its speed and strength, the Tryostronix is a highly advantageous hunter.

name: Tsintamoth
description: The Tsintamoth inherited its large cranial crest from its hybrid parent Tsintaosaurus. This crest gives the Tsintamoth's trumpeting calls a unique range of pitches that confuse other pachiderms. Members of the same Tsintamoth herd, however, can recognize these calls from miles away!

name: Tsintaosaurus
description: The special forward-facing crest on top of Tsintaosaurus' head has given it great scientific popularity. Aside from its crest, this creature was like most Hadrosaur, because it could move between quadrupedal and bipedal stances and was a target for large predators like T. rex.

name: Tuojiangosaurus
description: This Stegosaurid has the most impressive dorsal plates of any member of its species. Their height and points often distract enemies in battle, making this dinosaur seem larger than it really is.

name: Tuoramoloch
description: It's believed the enormous defensive spikes of this superhybrid may also have sensory capabilities similar to the spines of a sea urchin. The purpose of such a trait is still unknown.

name: Tupandactylus
description: Many scientists believe the large cranial crest of the Tupandactylus was used to signal other members of the species. This is due to the fact that the crests grow differently on males and females.

name: Tyrannodactylus
description: This armored theropod uses not only its jaws, but its back and shoulders in attacks on prey!

name: Tyrannolophosaur
description: The Tyrannolophosaur's lighter frame is better built for running than the T. rex. Its smaller head and shorter neck also allow for more agility while hunting when compared to other large theropods. Given its stalking prowess, many have compared this predatory hybrid to the Persian leopard.

name: Tyrannometrodon
description: The weight of Tyrannometrodon's dorsal sail and thick skull, both inherited from its Dimetrodon ancestor, make this hybrid significantly slower than other Tyrannosaurs. It specializes in preying on large, slow moving herbivores.

name: Tyrannosaur Buck
description: This male T. rex was once hunted by the big game hunter Roland Tembo on Isla Sorna! Tranquilized by Tembo and brought to San Diego, this T. rex escaped and was let loose in the city!

name: Tyrannosaur Doe
description: This female T. rex is the mate of Tyrannosaur Buck! Can you help her find her offspring, Junior?

name: Tyrannosaurus Rex
description: The bite of a T. rex is unmatched. This dinosaur chomps with enough force to crush bone.

name: Tyrannosaurus Rex Gen 2
description: Like other Tyrannosaurids, this GEN 2 clone is instinct driven, and attracted to sounds and movements. This makes it more easy to find than some other large theropods.

name: Utahraptor
description: The Utahraptor is the largest and most aggressive of all raptor species. It uses quick, slashing strikes with the long claws on its feet.

name: Utarinex
description: This fusion of the Dracorex and Utahsinoraptor matches the intelligence of the Raptor with the defensive capablity of the Dracorex and Sinoceratops. Its unique coloring likely helps to intimidate its opponents.

name: Utasinoraptor
description: The herbivorous DNA of the Sinoceratops does nothing to quell the savagery of this hybrid. Stockier in build than the Pyroraptor, the Utasinoraptor is as good a hunter as it is a scavenger.

name: Vectispinus
description: The 'White Rock spinosaurid' Vectispinus is among the largest theropods to ever walk the Earth!

name: Velociraptor
description: Jurassic World's geneticists made their Velociraptor far larger than its counterparts in the fossil record. This powerhouse pack predator is the wolf of Jurassic World.

name: Velosrhacos
description: Velosrhacos’ fusion includes DNA from an IBRIS Project experiment that had impressive problem-solving skills. Those skills were transferred to Velosrhacos, making her a legend on the hunting scene.

name: Woolly Mammoth
description: The Woolly Mammoth’s tusks have a more defined curve than any modern elephant. They are used as weapons in combat, and can grow to nearly the same length as a mid-size car!

name: Woolly Rhino
description: A frigid desert environment is the Woolly Rhinoceros’ preferred habitat. This creature has a thick coat, and a large back hump filled with fat to keep it nourished when resources are limited.

name: Wuerhosaurus
description: This relative of the Stegosaurus uses similar attacks and defenses against predators. You can tell the two dinosaurs apart by the Wuerhosaurus's slightly smaller build and more rectangular plates.

name: Yutyrannus
description: The feathered tyrant Yutyrannus is the largest known carnivorous dinosaur which definitely had feathers!

name: Yuxisaurus
description: Yuxisaurus means 'Yuxi lizard,' named for the Yuxi locality of China where its fossils were found. Discovery of the Yuxisaurus in 2021 proved the presence of the arrmored ornithischian dinosaurs 'thyreophora' in Asia!
